data.content:
### Customer Support Agent Setup and Development

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/official-documentation/Anthropic-Quickstarts/CLAUDE.md

Commands for installing dependencies, running the development server, and building the Customer Support Agent project. Supports different UI variants.

```shell
npm install
```

```shell
npm run dev
```

```shell
npm run dev:left
```

```shell
npm run dev:right
```

```shell
npm run dev:chat
```

```shell
npm run lint
```

```shell
npm run build
```

--------------------------------

### Financial Data Analyst Setup and Development

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/official-documentation/Anthropic-Quickstarts/CLAUDE.md

Commands for installing dependencies, running the development server, linting, and building the Financial Data Analyst project.

```shell
npm install
```

```shell
npm run dev
```

```shell
npm run lint
```

```shell
npm run build
```

--------------------------------

### Computer-Use Demo Setup and Development

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/official-documentation/Anthropic-Quickstarts/CLAUDE.md

Commands for setting up the environment, building a Docker image, and running the Computer-Use Demo container. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.

```shell
./setup.sh
```

```shell
docker build . -t computer-use-demo:local
```

```shell
docker run -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -v $(pwd)/computer_use_demo:/home/computeruse/computer_use_demo/ -v $HOME/.anthropic:/home/computeruse/.anthropic -p 5900:5900 -p 8501:8501 -p 6080:6080 -p 8080:8080 -it computer-use-demo:local
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Installs project dependencies using pip. Assumes a pyproject.toml file is present for dependency management.

```bash
pip install -e .
```

--------------------------------

### Run Perplexity MCP Server

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Starts the Perplexity MCP Server using Node.js.

```bash
node server.js
```

--------------------------------

### Initial Setup: Populate Processed List

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Initializes the processed repository list by running the script with the --init flag. This ensures that only new additions trigger notifications, not existing entries.

```python
python scripts/badge_issue_notification.py --init
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Installs the necessary Node.js dependencies for the Perplexity MCP Server.

```bash
npm install
```

--------------------------------

### Install Beta/RC Version

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Basic-Memory/CLAUDE.md

Installs a pre-release version (beta or release candidate) of the basic-memory package from PyPI. This is typically used for milestone testing before a stable release.

```bash
pip install basic-memory --pre
```

--------------------------------

### Computer-Use Demo Testing and Code Quality

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/official-documentation/Anthropic-Quickstarts/CLAUDE.md

Commands for linting, formatting, type checking, and running tests for the Computer-Use Demo project using Ruff and Pyright.

```shell
ruff check .
```

```shell
ruff format .
```

```shell
pyright
```

```shell
pytest
```

```shell
pytest tests/path_to_test.py::test_name -v
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Desktop Configuration (Global Install)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Configures the Perplexity MCP server in Claude Desktop after a global npm installation. Uses 'npx' to execute the installed package.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "perplexity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "perplexity-mcp"
      ],
      "env": {
        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY": "your_perplexity_api_key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Python Best Practices Examples

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/AWS-MCP-Server/CLAUDE.md

Illustrates Python best practices such as using pathlib, logging, specific exceptions, comprehensions, dataclasses, and environment variables.

```python
from pathlib import Path
import logging

# Prefer pathlib over os.path
log_file = Path("logs/app.log")

# Use logging instead of print
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
logging.info("Application started")

# Use specific exceptions
class CustomError(Exception):
    pass

try:
    # Some operation that might fail
    result = 10 / 0
except ZeroDivisionError as e:
    logging.error(f"Division by zero error: {e}", exc_info=True)

# Use comprehensions
squares = [x**2 for x in range(10)]

# Use dataclasses
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class User:
    name: str
    user_id: int

# Use environment variables (e.g., with python-dotenv)
# import os
# api_key = os.getenv("API_KEY")
```

--------------------------------

### Install Development Version

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Basic-Memory/CLAUDE.md

Installs the latest development version of the basic-memory package from PyPI. The --pre flag is used to allow pre-release versions, and --force-reinstall ensures the latest version is used.

```bash
pip install basic-memory --pre --force-reinstall
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies with pip

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/AWS-MCP-Server/CLAUDE.md

Installs project dependencies and development dependencies using pip.

```bash
pip install -e .
pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

--------------------------------

### NPM Global Installation

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Installs the Perplexity MCP server globally using npm. This allows it to be run directly via 'npx'.

```bash
npm install -g .
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Test Commands

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Basic-Memory/CLAUDE.md

Commands for installing dependencies, running tests, linting, type checking, formatting, and performing all code checks. Also includes commands for database migration and running the MCP Inspector.

```bash
just install
pip install -e ".[dev]"
uv run pytest -p pytest_mock -v
just test
pytest tests/path/to/test_file.py::test_function_name
just lint
ruff check . --fix
just type-check
uv run pyright
just format
uv run ruff format .
just check
just migration "Your migration message"
just run-inspector
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Desktop Configuration (Local Install)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Configures the Perplexity MCP server in Claude Desktop when installed locally. Specifies the command to run the server script and its environment variables.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "perplexity": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": [
        "/absolute/path/to/perplexity-mcp/server.js"
      ],
      "env": {
        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY": "your_perplexity_api_key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies with uv

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/AWS-MCP-Server/CLAUDE.md

Installs project dependencies and development dependencies using the uv package manager. It also covers updating and syncing from a lock file.

```bash
uv pip install --system -e .
uv pip install --system -e ".[dev]"
uv pip compile --system pyproject.toml -o uv.lock
uv pip sync --system uv.lock
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Token (.env File)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Creates a .env file to store the GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT). This is the recommended method for local testing and is gitignored for security.

```bash
echo "AWESOME_CC_PAT_PUBLIC_REPO=your_github_personal_access_token" > .env
```

--------------------------------

### Conventional Commits Examples

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Course-Builder/CLAUDE.md

Examples of conventional commit messages used in the project, demonstrating the format `<type>(<scope>): <description>` with various types and scopes for apps and packages.

```javascript
// fix(egh): convert SanityReference to SanityArrayElementReference
// style(mdx-mermaid): make flowcharts nicer
// refactor(utils): implement SEO utility package with re-export pattern
// feat(utils-email): create email utilities package with sendAnEmail
```

--------------------------------

### Project Structure Example

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/claude-code-mcp-enhanced/CLAUDE.md

Illustrates the expected directory and file structure for the project, including source code, documentation, examples, and tests.

```plaintext
project_name/
├── docs/
│   ├── CHANGELOG.md
│   ├── memory_bank/
│   └── tasks/
├── examples/
├── pyproject.toml
├── README.md
├── src/
│   └── project_name/
├── tests/
│   ├── fixtures/
│   └── project_name/
└── uv.lock
```

--------------------------------

### Build Commands for Guitar Project

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Guitar/CLAUDE.md

Provides essential commands for setting up dependencies, preparing the environment, and building the Guitar project in both release and debug modes.

```bash
# Setup dependencies
sudo apt install build-essential ruby qmake6 libqt6core6 libqt6gui6 libqt6svg6-dev libqt6core5compat6-dev zlib1g-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libssl-dev
```

```ruby
# Prepare environment
ruby prepare.rb
```

```bash
# Build project
mkdir build && cd build && qmake6 ../Guitar.pro && make -j8
```

```bash
# Release build
qmake "CONFIG+=release" ../Guitar.pro && make
```

```bash
# Debug build
qmake "CONFIG+=debug" ../Guitar.pro && make
```

--------------------------------

### Anthropic Quickstarts

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/README.md

Comprehensive development guides for three AI-powered demo projects using Claude Code. Includes standardized workflows, code style guidelines, and containerization instructions.

```markdown
[Anthropic Quickstarts](https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-quickstarts/blob/main/CLAUDE.md) by [Anthropic](https://github.com/anthropics)
```

--------------------------------

### Perplexity MCP Server Tools

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Lists the available tools for interacting with the Perplexity MCP Server.

```APIDOC
Tools:

- perplexity_ask: Send a single question to Perplexity.
  - Default model: `llama-3.1-sonar-small-128k-online`

- perplexity_chat: Multi-turn conversation with Perplexity.
  - Default model: `mixtral-8x7b-instruct`
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Test Commands

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/TPL/CLAUDE.md

Common commands for building, formatting, and testing the TPL-GO project. These commands leverage `make` for convenience and standard Go tooling.

```bash
make
make deps
make test
go test -v ./...
go test -v -run=TestName
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Testing: Dry Run

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Runs the script in a dry run mode, which simulates the processing of entries without actually creating GitHub issues. Useful for testing the detection logic.

```python
python scripts/badge_issue_notification.py
```

--------------------------------

### Initial Project Context Setup

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/README.md

Sets up initial project context by viewing directory structure and reading key files, creating standardized context with directory visualization and key documentation focus.

```markdown
`/prime` by `yzyydev`
Sets up initial project context by viewing directory structure and reading key files, creating standardized context with directory visualization and key documentation focus.
```

--------------------------------

### Versioning with Git Tags

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/AWS-MCP-Server/CLAUDE.md

Demonstrates how to use Git tags for versioning releases and how setuptools_scm automatically determines versions for development.

```bash
# For releases:
git tag -a 1.2.3 -m "Release 1.2.3"
git push origin 1.2.3

# For development, setuptools_scm automatically determines versions from Git tags.
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Memory MCP Project Management Tools

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Basic-Memory/CLAUDE.md

Tools for managing projects within Basic Memory, including listing, switching, getting current project status, creating, deleting, setting defaults, and checking sync status.

```APIDOC
list_memory_projects() -> list[dict]
  - Lists all available projects with status indicators.
  - Returns: A list of dictionaries, each representing a project.

switch_project(project_name: str)
  - Switches to a different project context during conversations.
  - Parameters:
    - project_name (str): The name of the project to switch to.

get_current_project() -> dict
  - Shows the currently active project with statistics.
  - Returns: A dictionary containing information about the current project.

create_memory_project(name: str, path: str, set_default: bool = False)
  - Creates new Basic Memory projects.
  - Parameters:
    - name (str): The name of the new project.
    - path (str): The file system path for the project.
    - set_default (bool, optional): Whether to set this project as the default. Defaults to False.

delete_project(name: str)
  - Deletes projects from configuration and database.
  - Parameters:
    - name (str): The name of the project to delete.

set_default_project(name: str)
  - Sets the default project in the configuration.
  - Parameters:
    - name (str): The name of the project to set as default.

sync_status() -> dict
  - Checks file synchronization status and background operations.
  - Returns: A dictionary with synchronization status information.
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Development Commands

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Course-Builder/CLAUDE.md

Commands for building and running the project in development mode. Supports building all packages/apps or specific ones using filters.

```bash
pnpm build:all
pnpm build
pnpm build --filter="ai-hero"
pnpm dev:all
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Build/Test/Lint Commands (Poetry)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Lamoom-Python/CLAUDE.md

Commands for installing dependencies, running tests (all, specific, with coverage), and formatting code using Poetry.

```shell
poetry install
poetry run pytest --cache-clear -vv tests
poetry run pytest tests/path/to/test_file.py::test_function_name -v
make test
make format
```

--------------------------------

### TSDoc Comments for Utility Functions

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Course-Builder/CLAUDE.md

Provides a template for comprehensive TSDoc comments for utility functions, including a brief description, additional details, parameter descriptions, return value descriptions, and usage examples.

```typescript
/**
 * Brief description of what the function does
 * 
 * Additional details if needed
 * 
 * @param paramName - Description of the parameter
 * @returns Description of the return value
 * 
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * // Example usage code
 * const result = myFunction('input')
 * ```
 */
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Development Commands

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Giselle/CLAUDE.md

Commands for building all packages, specific packages, checking types, formatting code, and running the development server.

```pnpm
pnpm build
pnpm build-sdk
pnpm build-data-type
pnpm check-types
pnpm -F <modified files packagename> check-types
pnpm format
pnpm dev
pnpm dev:studio.giselles.ai
```

--------------------------------

### Testing Browser Commands with vibe-tools

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Cursor-Tools/CLAUDE.md

Instructions for setting up and running browser command tests. This involves starting a test server, placing HTML files in a specific directory, and running test commands.

```bash
# Start the test server
pnpm serve-test

# Access test files at http://localhost:3000/filename.html

# Kill a process on port 3000 if it's busy
lsof -i :3000 | grep LISTEN | awk '{print $2}' | xargs kill

# Run test commands with latest code
pnpm dev browser <commands>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Token (Environment Variable)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Sets the GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) as an environment variable for local testing. This token is required for creating issues in external repositories.

```bash
export AWESOME_CC_PAT_PUBLIC_REPO=your_github_personal_access_token
```

--------------------------------

### Go Code Style and Conventions

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/TPL/CLAUDE.md

Guidelines for writing idiomatic Go code within the TPL-GO project. Covers formatting, naming, commenting, and import organization.

```go
// Use PascalCase for exported types/methods, camelCase for variables
// Add comments for public API and complex logic
// Group imports: standard library first, then third-party
```

--------------------------------

### Perplexity MCP Installation and Configuration

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/README.md

Offers clear step-by-step installation instructions with multiple configuration options, detailed troubleshooting guidance, and concise architecture overview of the MCP protocol.

```Markdown
Offers clear step-by-step installation instructions with multiple configuration options, detailed troubleshooting guidance, and concise architecture overview of the MCP protocol.
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Testing: Skip Issue Creation

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Executes the script while skipping the actual creation of GitHub issues. This is achieved by setting the CREATE_ISSUES environment variable to false, allowing for testing of the repository processing logic.

```python
export CREATE_ISSUES=false
python scripts/badge_issue_notification.py
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Desktop Configuration (NVM Users)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Configuration for NVM users, requiring absolute paths for both the Node.js executable and the server script.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "perplexity": {
      "command": "/Users/username/.nvm/versions/node/v16.x.x/bin/node",
      "args": [
        "/Users/username/path/to/perplexity-mcp/server.js"
      ],
      "env": {
        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY": "your_perplexity_api_key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting: View Logs

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Command to view the log file for the Perplexity MCP server.

```bash
cat ~/.claude/logs/perplexity.log
```

--------------------------------

### Go Testing Strategies

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/TPL/CLAUDE.md

Best practices for writing tests in Go, emphasizing table-driven tests, clear expectations, and comprehensive error case testing.

```go
// Write table-driven tests with clear input/output expectations
// Use package tpl_test for external testing perspective
// Include detailed error messages (expected vs. actual)
// Test every exported function and error case
```
